title: Decode String
description:
Given an encoded string, return the decoded string.

The encoding rule: `k[encoded_string]` means `encoded_string` repeated exactly `k` times. You may assume the input is always valid; there are no extra spaces, brackets are well-formed, etc.

Nesting is allowed: `"3[a2[c]]"` → `"accaccacc"`.
hints: Use a stack to handle nesting — push current string and count when you see '['.
When you see ']', pop and repeat the current string.
Build numbers character by character (they can be multi-digit).
